item.title: To negotiate with Iran, understand it first
item.description: Western countries assume Iran’s actions are aimed at them. But something else is going on.

item.title: By undermining elections, Trump threatens democracy. Look at Russia.
item.description: Meanwhile, Ukraine vigorously defends democracy on and off the battlefield.

item.title: Culture is worth the war
item.description: The vicious battles over art and politics started long ago. I’m glad they’re back.

item.title: Vaccines are a blessing. Let’s not forget why.
item.description: The last American who used an iron lung just died. Her story should be a reminder.

item.title: The day a congregation turned on the pastor of a South Carolina church
item.description: Faith calls for forgiveness, but protecting children demands accountability in Pawleys Island.

item.title: Why homeownership feels impossible for Gen Z
item.description:
For generations, owning a home was seen as the first real step into adulthood: a place to raise a family, build wealth and claim a piece of the American Dream. But for Gen Z, soaring prices, high mortgage rates and a shortage of starter homes have pushed even modest houses out of reach.
Host Megan McArdle argues that young Americans really have received a worse housing deal than earlier generations. But the era of cheap, abundant homes to buy was shaped by forces we cannot simply recreate, and a good life does not have to begin with buying the perfect house.

item.title: Nuclear dominoes
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Saudi Arabia, Iran, nuclear deal.

item.title: How the Greenland standoff pushed Europe to prep for war with the U.S.
item.description: President Trump threatened to take the island by force. America’s NATO allies called his bluff.

item.title: Newsom has pushed California to a tipping point
item.description: Is the United States turning into the Golden State? In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” Kate Andrews talks with historian Victor Davis Hanson about the political and cultural shifts reshaping America. They discuss Christopher Nolan’s take on “The Odyssey,” the war with Iran, Gavin Newsom’s record in California, and whether democratic socialism is a genuine grassroots movement or a project driven by urban elites.

item.title: The campaign against higher ed strikes again. This time, it hit me.
item.description: Post readers on academia and public trust

item.title: ‘There I sat, delighted’: Are movies changing for good?
item.description: This summer’s box office hits haven’t all been predictable.

item.title: The World Cup that restored my faith in America
item.description: Not even Trumpian meddling could dampen a nationwide celebration.

item.title: The problem with RFK Jr.’s crusade against antidepressants
item.description: Millions of Americans rely on these medications. This is the wrong way to change policy on them.

item.title: Cricket gets a political makeover in Modi’s India
item.description: The sport that long helped unite the region is now governed by the politics of the day.

item.title: Trump’s generic drug tariffs will drive up pharmaceutical prices
If he’d tried to arrest Netanyahu, Mamdani would be the one in jail
item.description: New York’s mayor backed off his campaign pledge. Federal law explains why.

item.title: Democrats are hoping for a Nebraska or Montana Senate surprise
item.description: Don’t bet on it, but the good news is they don’t need them to win the Senate.
